name: downloads
context: Handle browser-triggered file downloads with waitForEvent, suggested filenames, saveAs, cleanup, and CI artifact retention.
info: Downloads in Playwright are event-driven browser artifacts. This context teaches agents to start waiting before clicking, use the download object's suggested filename, save files before the context closes, validate MIME/content when needed, and manage artifacts safely in local and CI runs. Agents use it when automating export buttons, invoice downloads, generated reports, CSV workflows, PDFs, or any UI action where the browser receives a file rather than rendering content.

sections.title: Event order
sections.body: Always create `const downloadPromise = page.waitForEvent('download')` before triggering the download. If the click happens first, a fast download event can be missed. This pattern mirrors `waitForResponse` and other event waits.

sections.title: Save and verify
sections.body: Use `download.suggestedFilename()` for the browser-provided name and `download.saveAs(path)` to persist the file. After saving, verify existence, file size, extension, MIME-adjacent content, or parse the file directly for assertions. For CSV, parse rows; for PDF, at least verify magic bytes or use a PDF parser when content matters.

sections.title: Artifact management
sections.body: Write downloads under `artifacts/`, `test-results/`, or a CI artifact path. Do not commit generated downloads unless they are intentional fixtures. Review authenticated downloads for secrets or personal data before uploading them to CI artifacts or issue trackers.

sections.title: Automation fit
sections.body: Use browser downloads when validating the UI flow matters. If only the file endpoint matters, use [[playwright/api-testing]] or [[curl/request]] to call it directly with the same auth headers or storage-derived session cookies.

links.context: Authenticated exports usually need storage state
Automate the UI flow that triggers downloads
Call download endpoints directly when browser UI is irrelevant
Verify saved files and manage artifact paths

related.note: Downloaded files are verified and moved with filesystem APIs.
Direct request contexts are faster when UI behavior does not matter.
CI workflows often upload downloaded artifacts for debugging.

author: dojo-community
